title: [Proposal] Fix Telegram Proposal Approve Button Not Executing Proposals
prompt:

resolve to workspace, not src/, and will FAIL with "file not found". Use ONLY:

  • read_source(file, start_line?, num_lines?)               — read src/ file

  • grep_source(pattern, path?)                              — search src/
  • find_replace_source(file, find, replace)                 — text swap
  • replace_lines_source(file, start_line, end_line, new_content) — line edit
  • insert_after_source(file, after_line, content)           — insert lines

  • write_source(file, content, overwrite?)                  — create/overwrite src file
TITLE: Fix Telegram Proposal Approve Button Not Executing Proposals
PRIORITY: critical
TYPE: src_edit
SUMMARY: Telegram proposal approval callback approves proposals in the store but never dispatches execution. The button confirms approval but the executor never runs.
FULL IMPLEMENTATION PLAN:
## Why this change

When a user clicks the ✅ Approve button on a proposal in Telegram, the callback handler (`approveProposal()`) updates the proposal status to "approved" in the store and sends a confirmation message, but it **never calls `dispatchApprovedProposal()`** — the function responsible for actually triggering the executor agent.

Comparison: The HTTP endpoint handler (proposals.router.ts lines 119-145) correctly calls `dispatchApprovedProposal()` on line 136 after approval. The Telegram handler is missing this critical step.

## Exact source edits

**File**: src/gateway/telegram-handler.ts
**Lines**: 974-1006 (the `approveProposal()` callback handler)

**Current code** (lines 994-1000):
```typescript
await this.proposalsStore.approveProposal(proposalId);

// Update the button to show approved
button.text = '✅ Approved';
button.callback_data = `proposal_approved:${proposalId}`;

await this.telegramApi.editMessageReplyMarkup(chatId, messageId, {
  inline_keyboard: [[button]]
});

await this.telegramApi.sendMessage(chatId, `✅ Proposal approved. Executor will begin shortly.`);
```

**Fixed code** (add after line 999, before the success message):
```typescript
await this.proposalsStore.approveProposal(proposalId);

// Update the button to show approved
button.text = '✅ Approved';
button.callback_data = `proposal_approved:${proposalId}`;

await this.telegramApi.editMessageReplyMarkup(chatId, messageId, {
  inline_keyboard: [[button]]
});

// CRITICAL: Dispatch the approved proposal to the executor
await dispatchApprovedProposal(proposalId);

await this.telegramApi.sendMessage(chatId, `✅ Proposal approved. Executor will begin shortly.`);
```

## Deterministic behavior after patch

1. User clicks ✅ Approve button in Telegram
2. Proposal status changes to "approved" in store
3. **`dispatchApprovedProposal(proposalId)` is called immediately**
4. Executor agent spawns with the proposal instructions
5. Confirmation message sent to Telegram with status update
6. Executor runs and completes the task (or reports errors)

## Acceptance tests

1. Create a test proposal via Telegram
2. Click the ✅ Approve button
3. Verify in `task_control(action: 'list')` that a new task was created with the executor agent
4. Verify the executor completes the task (check logs or task status)
5. Verify the confirmation message was sent to Telegram

## Risks and compatibility

- **Low risk**: This is a missing function call, not a structural change
- **No breaking changes**: The function already exists and is called by the HTTP handler successfully
- **Backward compatible**: Approved proposals will now execute as originally intended
- **Side effect**: Proposals will actually execute now (this is the desired behavior)


AFFECTED FILES:
  - [edit] src/gateway/telegram-handler.ts: Add dispatchApprovedProposal() call to the approval callback handler

BUILD REQUIRED: Yes — run npm run build after edits. MANDATORY. Stop if build fails — task will pause for assistance.
INSTRUCTIONS:
1. Read affected files with the correct source read tool first (read_source for src/, read_webui_source for web-ui/) to understand current state.
2. Apply edits using the source tool matching the target path and edit operation (src/*_source for src/, webui/*_webui_source for web-ui/).
3. For one-time parallel side work, use additive background primitives: background_spawn, background_status/background_progress, background_join.
4. Run: run_command({command:"npm run build", shell:true}). If it fails, STOP — do not retry.
5. Report exactly what was changed and any issues encountered.
ADDITIONAL EXECUTOR INSTRUCTIONS:
You are executing a code fix proposal. Read src/gateway/telegram-handler.ts lines 974-1006 to confirm the approveProposal() callback handler. Add the missing dispatchApprovedProposal(proposalId) call after the button is updated (line 999) and before the success message is sent (line 1000). Use find_replace_source to make this surgical edit. The function dispatchApprovedProposal already exists in the codebase — you are simply calling it where it was forgotten. After the edit is complete, call gateway_restart with a summary of the change.
Proposal ID: prop_1775337325204_2f4730

journal.detail:
STEP 2 PROGRESS: Identified the bug and prepared fix for Telegram proposal approval handler.

LOCATION: src/gateway/comms/telegram-channel.ts, lines 974-1006

BUG: When user clicks approve button on a proposal via Telegram (pr:ap action), the code calls approveProposal() but does NOT call dispatchApprovedProposal() to actually execute the proposal. This leaves proposals in "approved" state but never transitions them to "executing".

COMPARISON: The HTTP approval route (proposals.router.ts lines 119-145) CORRECTLY calls dispatchApprovedProposal() after approving. Telegram callback needs same logic.

REQUIRED CHANGES:
1. Import dispatchApprovedProposal from '../routes/proposals.router'
2. After approveProposal() succeeds, check if proposal has execution plan
3. If yes, call dispatchApprovedProposal(approved, { channel: 'telegram', telegramChatId: chatId })
4. Update user message to show execution is happening
5. Add error handling for dispatch failures

This matches the pattern already established in proposals.router.ts where HTTP approval also dispatches proposals asynchronously.
